title: Jurassic Park
description: One of the most beloved modern pinball machines. A choose-your-own-adventure through Isla Nublar — rescue workers, capture dinosaurs, and try not to get eaten. Deep rules but a satisfying core loop even if you only scratch the surface.

summary.text: Jurassic Park is a choose-your-own-adventure through a dinosaur-infested island. The playfield has a big map with branching paths — you literally drive a truck through paddocks, deciding to go left or right. The core loop is: spell M-A-P (hit white shots) to unlock your next paddock, rescue workers inside, set traps, call the helicopter, and capture the dinosaur. Meanwhile, bashing the truck spells T-R-E-X for T-Rex events, and spelling C-H-A-O-S through shot-truck combos starts a huge multiball. Three mini-wizard modes lead to the final wizard mode. There's an absurd amount of depth here, but the beautiful thing is: you can ignore most of it and just focus on capturing dinosaurs and have a great game.

one_shot.name: The Left Ramp (T-Rex Mouth)
one_shot.description: The left ramp is the most important shot on this game. It starts paddock modes (when MAP is spelled and lit), it can be backhanded from the right flipper (easier on the Pro), and it feeds predictably. On the Premium/LE, the T-Rex head will sometimes come down and grab the ball — which is awesome but also means the shot behaves differently depending on game state. This is where you start your adventure. When MAP is spelled, shoot the left ramp with the truck pointed in the direction you want to go on the map. Kineticist's tip: stick to the edges of the map when playing competitively — it's a shorter path to the Visitor Center than going through the middle.

hard_box.text: This game has arguably the deepest ruleset of any modern pinball machine. There are paddock modes, T-Rex events, control room modes, Chaos multiball, Raptor tri-ball, supply drops, DNA combos, fossils, smart missiles, and three mini-wizard modes that feed into a final wizard mode. Nobody understands all of it. The layout can be punishing — the {{callout:o-shot}} is one of the tightest shots in modern pinball, the {{callout:right-ramp}} is at an awkward angle, and the truck captive ball can send balls sideways unpredictably. But here's the secret: the core loop (spell MAP → rescue workers → set traps → call helicopter → capture dinosaur) is simple and satisfying even if you never touch the advanced systems.

ball_plans.title: Spell MAP, Start Your First Paddock

priorities.title: Skill Shot — Left Ramp
priorities.description: The primary skill shot is the left ramp (the ramp with the T-Rex head above it). If you nail it, there's a progressive super skill shot chain: left ramp → right ramp → tower, rapid-fire. It's satisfying and scores well. Alternative opening: soft plunge to the flippers and try for the C target (the control room shot on the lower left) — getting an early C helps spell CHAOS later.

priorities.title: Spell M-A-P by Hitting White Shots
priorities.description: Look for the three shots with white-lit inserts labeled M, A, and P — these are scattered across the major shots and change position between games. Hit any shot that has a white M, A, or P insert lit in front of it. Three white shots and MAP is spelled, which unlocks your first paddock at the left ramp. You'll often get M and A just from the ball bouncing around after the plunge. MAP progress carries over between balls, so don't stress if you only get one or two letters.

priorities.title: Point the Truck, Start Your First Paddock
priorities.description: Once MAP is spelled, the left ramp starts a paddock — but which paddock depends on which way the {{callout:truck}} is pointing. The truck is a captive ball mechanism in the center-right of the playfield; hit it to swing it left or right. Left truck = left path on the island map, right truck = right path. Check the truck direction before shooting the ramp. Stick to the edges of the map — the center paddocks are harder and the edges are a shorter path to the Visitor Center.

priorities.title: Inside the Paddock: The Rescue Loop
priorities.description: Once you're in a paddock, the game walks you through a sequence. First: hit the flashing green shots to rescue workers (the green lights show where people need saving). Second: once you've rescued enough, yellow-lit standup targets appear — hit those to set your traps. Third: shoot the {{callout:helipad}} on the far right to call the helicopter. Finally: look for the green arrow — that's the captured dinosaur. Shoot it to complete the paddock. The first paddock only requires one rescue and one trap, so it goes fast.

priorities.title: Bash the Truck Toward T-R-E-X
priorities.description: Every hit to the {{callout:truck}} captive ball earns a letter in T-R-E-X (shown in the top-middle of the display). Spelling it starts a T-Rex event — the first one is T-Rex Multiball, a 2-ball multiball where you hit red shots for jackpots and shoot the T-Rex mouth for super jackpots. You'll hit the truck incidentally just from playing. It's not worth a lot on its own, but it's a free multiball you can use as a safety net for other modes.

ball_plans.title: Capture Dinosaurs, Build Toward Control Room

priorities.title: Keep Running Paddocks
priorities.description: Spell MAP again by hitting shots with white M-A-P inserts lit in front of them, then shoot the left ramp to start your next paddock. Same rescue loop: hit green shots to rescue workers → hit yellow trap targets → shoot the helipad → capture the dinosaur at the green arrow. Later paddocks require more rescues and the dinosaurs move toward your workers faster, but the sequence is always the same. Each captured dinosaur gives you a perk and progresses your position on the island map toward the Visitor Center.

priorities.title: Start a Control Room Mode
priorities.description: After completing paddocks, control room modes light at the {{callout:c-shot}} (the target on the lower left — a post pops up to catch your ball). There are three modes you'll play in order: Virus Attack (hit blue-lit shots, cash out at the control room target 3 times), Restore Power (hit the spinner to prime the system, then hit blue shots), and System Boot (a combo chain — your first shot determines what your next combo shot is). You start them by hitting the C target when its insert is lit.

priorities.title: Work the C-H-A-O-S Letters
priorities.description: To spell C-H-A-O-S, you alternate between hitting any major shot and then hitting the truck — shot, truck, shot, truck. Each pair earns a letter. It's shown on the playfield inserts near the truck. Once all five letters are spelled, the {{callout:smart-missile}} target lights to start Chaos Multiball. In the multiball, hit red shots to build value, then hit the truck to collect chaos letters. It's a long, fun multiball that can stack with paddock modes.

priorities.title: Work Toward Raptor Tri-Ball
priorities.description: The {{callout:raptor-pen}} has a gate that starts up. Hit the targets behind it to lower the gate, then shoot through the opening to lock balls behind it. When the lightning bolt inserts near the gate start flashing intensely, one more bash starts the multiball. System-J's tip: when you're cradled on the left flipper with nothing else lit, backhand the raptor pen — it's one of the more valuable multiballs and backhanding is safer than a direct shot.

priorities.title: Complete Your Path to the Visitor Center
priorities.description: You need to reach the Visitor Center on the island map to qualify the Escape Nublar wizard mode. Keep spelling MAP (hit white M-A-P shots) and running the rescue loop in each paddock. If you've been sticking to the edge paths on the map, you might be close. Every captured dinosaur also gives you a perk — capturing the T-Rex increases all timers and awards an extra ball.

priorities.title: Chase Whichever Mini-Wizard Is Closest
priorities.description: Three mini-wizard modes exist: Secure Control Room (complete all 3 control room modes), Museum Mayhem (complete all T-Rex events), and Visitor Center (capture enough paddock dinosaurs to reach it on the map). You don't need all three for Escape Nublar — just reaching Visitor Center is enough. But completing the others first qualifies the ultimate wizard mode: When Dinosaurs Ruled the Earth.

priorities.title: Recognize 2X Scoring When It Happens
priorities.description: The far-right supply drop target lights 2x scoring for everything on the playfield. During multiball, balls bouncing into the three supply drop standup targets can randomly qualify this. When 2x is active, whatever you're doing is worth double — modes, jackpots, rescue streaks, all of it. You can't easily chase it, but you can recognize it when it lights and make your next shots count.

items.title: Escape Outlane (Left) — Earned Ball Save
items.description: The left outlane has a ball save, but it's not lit by default. Hit the Escape target (a standup target on the lower left side) enough times to light it. Keep track of whether it's lit — when it goes out, relight it before doing anything risky.

items.title: Right Outlane — Standard Setup
items.description: Standard inlane/outlane on the right side. There's a post near the left ramp entrance that pops up during certain game states (like starting modes) and can redirect balls. Be aware of it — it can save you or surprise you.

items.title: The Truck Will Betray You
items.description: The truck captive ball sends balls in unexpected directions. It also physically blocks the targets behind it depending on which way it's facing. Know which direction the truck is pointing before you bash it, and be ready for the ball to come back at an angle you didn't expect.

items.title: Stage Flipping (Upper Flipper Control)
items.description: The right flipper button controls both the lower and upper right flippers. Press halfway for the lower flipper, press fully for the upper. This means you can cradle a ball on the lower right flipper and independently use the upper flipper to hit shots. This is a key skill for T-Rex multiball — cradle one ball, aim the other with the upper flipper.

items.title: Helipad Aim
items.description: The helipad shot (far right) feeds down to the right flipper. It's also where new balls plunge. To hit it during paddock mode, try a late flip from the left flipper — that angle works more consistently than trying to aim it from the right.

items.title: Left Ramp and Tower as Bailouts
items.description: The left ramp feeds predictably and the tower shot (a quick 180 right of the spinner) feeds a left wireform that returns to the flippers. Both are relatively safe when you're unsure. The spinner up the middle can be hit from either flipper and feeds to the right side.

items.title: The O shot in pressure situations
items.description: The O shot on the right side is arguably the tightest shot in modern pinball. Aiming tip: from a left flipper cradle, aim for the light brown area of the Jurassic Park insignia above the flippers. Worth practicing, but don't go for it when you're behind or on Ball 3 with no ball save.

items.title: DNA Combos and Fossils (for now)
items.description: DNA combos are specific shot sequences (left ramp → right ramp → supply drop, etc.) that collect fossils for perks. Cool system, but it's a side quest built on top of a side quest. Let fossils accumulate naturally from completing modes and multiballs. Don't chase them.

items.title: The Smart Missile shot on Ball 1
items.description: Threading the needle past the truck to hit the amber mine target is one of the most satisfying shots in pinball. It's also one of the hardest. For advanced players, mastering this shot bridges the gap between good and great scores. For us, it's a Ball 2 or Ball 3 shot when we're feeling confident — not a Ball 1 priority.

items.title: Chasing When Dinosaurs Ruled the Earth
items.description: The ultimate wizard mode requires completing all three mini-wizard modes and then beating Escape Nublar. In competition, focus on reaching the Visitor Center and getting Escape Nublar lit. That alone is a massive achievement.

items.title: Center-of-the-map paddock paths
items.description: The center paddocks include the Spitter and the T-Rex, which are harder to complete and have more aggressive dinosaurs. Edge paths are faster and easier — point your truck accordingly when starting each paddock.

between_us.label: Good Scores to Aim For
between_us.text: This game's been out since 2019, so the community has it figured out. A solid tournament game is 500M-1B. Breaking 1 billion is a great achievement. The top players push into multi-billion territory with heavy mode stacking. For league night, consistently hitting 500M+ puts you in competitive territory.

between_us.label: The Core Loop Is Simple (Everything Else Is Extra)
between_us.text: Here's the thing about Jurassic Park: you can ignore most of the advanced systems and just focus on capturing dinosaurs and have a great time. The paddock loop (spell MAP → rescue → set traps → helipad → capture) is the backbone. T-Rex events, control room modes, Chaos multiball, fossils, DNA combos — these are all layers on top. Learn the paddock loop first. Everything else opens up naturally as you get comfortable.

between_us.label: Pro vs. Premium/LE
between_us.text: On the Pro: the left ramp is easier to backhand, the T-Rex doesn't grab balls (no motorized head), and the raptor pen gate works differently. The core strategy is identical. Premium/LE adds the T-Rex ball grab mechanic (cool but can change ball flow), the interactive raptor pen lock, and some shot differences.

between_us.label: Co-Op Mode Exists
between_us.text: Hold the action button before starting a game to access co-op mode — you and another player work through the adventure together. Also available: team play and the Escape Nublar challenge mode (final wizard mode as a timed standalone). Great for league nights with a partner or for practicing the endgame.

playfield_callouts.name: Left Ramp (T-Rex Mouth)
playfield_callouts.description: The most important shot on the game. Starts paddock modes when MAP is lit. On Premium/LE, the T-Rex head can come down and grab the ball. Backhanded more easily on the Pro. This is where your adventure begins.

playfield_callouts.name: Truck (Captive Balls / Map Navigator)
playfield_callouts.description: Center-right of the playfield. Two captive balls that swing the truck around. The truck direction determines which paddock path you take (left or right on the map). Each hit earns a T-R-E-X letter. Also blocks/reveals the targets behind it depending on direction. Check which way it's pointing before you bash it.

playfield_callouts.name: C Shot / Control Room
playfield_callouts.description: Left side of the playfield, close to the flippers. A post pops up to grab the ball. Best hit from a right flipper cradle. Starts control room modes and contributes to spelling C-H-A-O-S. One of the first letters in CHAOS is the C, making this a natural early shot.

playfield_callouts.name: Raptor Pen
playfield_callouts.description: Left side, near the C shot. A gate covers the targets behind it. Hit targets to lower the gate, then shoot behind to lock balls. Bash the gate when lightning bolts flash to start Raptor Tri-Ball. A good default backhand from the left flipper when nothing else is lit.

playfield_callouts.name: Helipad
playfield_callouts.description: Far right of the playfield. Where balls plunge from, and where you call the helicopter during paddock modes. After setting traps, shoot here to bring the helicopter, then capture the dinosaur at the green arrow. Late flips from the left flipper work best for hitting this consistently.

playfield_callouts.name: Smart Missile / Amber Mine
playfield_callouts.description: Behind the truck, between the pop bumpers. The left ramp grabs the ball and gives you one shot to thread past the truck and hit this target. Aim for the second captive ball to thread the needle. One of the most satisfying shots in pinball when you hit it. Gives great awards.

playfield_callouts.name: O Shot
playfield_callouts.description: Right side of the playfield. The tightest shot in the game — arguably the tightest on any modern Stern. Needed for the upper loop. Aiming tip: from a left flipper cradle, aim for the light brown area of the Jurassic Park insignia above the flippers. Satisfying when you hit it, heartbreaking when you don't.

playfield_callouts.name: Right Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: Right side, awkward angle. Helixes down and feeds the upper right flipper (not back to your lower flippers). Advances toward Pteranodon Attack frenzy mode. Not a shot you'll go for often, but it's part of DNA combos and super skill shot progression.

playfield_callouts.name: Spinner
playfield_callouts.description: Center of the playfield, hittable from either flipper. Feeds to the right side. Great sound. Spinner value increases based on other shots hit since the last spin (tower, helipad, ramps light inserts in front of it). Used to prime the Restore Power control room mode.

sources.title: Joel / Flip N Out Pinball — Jurassic Park Tutorial Video (Transcript)
sources.description: Full walkthrough on Premium model. Shows complete paddock progression, all three control room modes, T-Rex multiball, Chaos multiball, Raptor tri-ball, skill shot progression, and the path to Escape Nublar. Primary source for gameplay flow narration.

sources.title: Kineticist — 'Rescue Mission' Jurassic Park Tutorial by Noah Crable
sources.description: Comprehensive written tutorial covering layout, all mode mechanics, paddock strategy, and tournament tips. Key insights: edge-of-map strategy, O shot aiming tip (light brown insignia area), smart missile targeting, helipad late-flip technique.

sources.title: System-J Pinball — Jurassic Park Tactics Page
sources.description: Excellent multi-layered strategy resource with newbie through advanced strategies. Key insights: soft plunge C shot opening, raptor pen backhand as default idle shot, T-Rex multiball stacking advice, smart missile timing, spinner value mechanics.

sources.title: Tilt Forums — Stern Jurassic Park Rulesheet
sources.description: Community wiki rulesheet with complete scoring data, paddock list, T-Rex event sequence, control room mode details, and strategy section.

sources.title: San Diego Pinball Club — Jurassic Park Cheatsheet
sources.description: Quick visual reference card for tournament play.

sources.title: Pinside — Jurassic Park Owners Club Thread
sources.description: Community strategy discussion including 2x scoring optimization, rescue streak value, stage flipping technique for upper flipper, and paddock path recommendations (avoid center paths).

sources.title: Stern Pinball — Jurassic Park Feature Matrix
sources.description: Manufacturer spec sheet with Pro/Premium/LE feature comparison, mech descriptions, and model differences.

chat_starters: What's the paddock loop and how do I start one?
Which direction should I point the truck?
How do I start Chaos Multiball?
What's the smart missile and should I go for it?

level_up.intro: Jurassic Park has one of the deepest rulesets in modern pinball. Once the paddock loop feels automatic, these strategies will take your scoring to the next level.

tips.title: Stack T-Rex Multiball With a Paddock Mode
tips.description: T-Rex Multiball doesn't score much on its own — but it's a free 2-ball multiball you can bring into a paddock mode. Spell T-R-E-X, start the multiball, then drain to single ball. Now start your paddock with the multiball as a safety net. System-J calls this out as a core intermediate strategy.

tips.title: Smart Missile Supremacy
tips.description: Kineticist calls mastering the smart missile shot the bridge between good and great scores. The left ramp grabs the ball and gives you one shot to thread past the truck into the amber mine. Aim for the second captive ball. The awards are substantial and include spotted rescues, add-a-balls, and major point bonuses. After 5+ rescues, Extra Ball becomes available as a smart missile award.

tips.title: Control Room Mode Stacking
tips.description: Control room modes can be started during paddock modes — Joel shows this happening naturally in his tutorial. If you have a control room mode ready and are about to start a paddock, start the paddock first, then shoot the C target to layer the control room mode on top. More shots lit = more things to hit during multiball.

tips.title: 2X Scoring Awareness
tips.description: The far right supply drop target lights 2x scoring for everything. In multiball, balls bouncing into supply drop targets can qualify your first supply drop, which adds a ball AND lights the 2x standup. The Pinside owners club says 2x scoring often starts 'somewhat serendipitously' — recognize it when it happens and make your next shots count double.

tips.title: Edge Path Strategy for Visitor Center
tips.description: The edges of the map are faster routes to the Visitor Center than the center. Kineticist specifically recommends this for competition play. The center paddocks (Spitter, T-Rex) are harder to complete. Plan your truck direction before each paddock to stay on the edges.

tips.title: Spinner Value Optimization
tips.description: System-J details this: spinner value increases based on other shots hit since the last spin. Tower, helipad, and both ramps light inserts in front of the spinner. If all are lit and you have super spinner available from the right inlane, the spinner becomes worth serious points. Most of the time it's not worth chasing, but in a tight tournament game, a fully-loaded spinner shot can be the difference.
